Video.summary: A remnant of the last Ice Age, the musk ox is almost mythical in appearance. For the people who share the Barren Ground region of Canada with them, they are an integral part of their culture. Show host, Dan Cabela, participates in a scientific sampling project to monitor the - population's health, and get first-hand experience of the relationship between local people and this arctic icon.
Video.title: "Barren Ground Musk Ox"

Video.summary: In Episode 3, Josh starts off in the Twin Cities where he meets up with Yia Vang, an award-winning Hmong chef and restaurant owner, bringing his family’s traditions and flavors to the American Midwest. As Josh heads up north to Duluth and the boundary waters, he’ll meet plenty of farmers and foragers—harvesting mushrooms, wild rice, and catching a few fish (and waves) along the way. It all wraps up with an epic feast hosted by Yia back in the cities.
Video.title: DIRT - "Can You Surf & Eat Like a Local in Minnesota? Wild Foraging & Delicious Local Eats"

Image.alt: DIRT - "Can You Surf & Eat Like a Local in Minnesota? Wild Foraging & Delicious Local Eats"
DIRT - "Can You Surf & Eat Like a Local in Minnesota? Wild Foraging & Delicious Local Eats"
DIRT - "Can You Surf & Eat Like a Local in Minnesota? Wild Foraging & Delicious Local Eats"

Video.summary: Capt. Jonathan Moss ventures to South Florida with the hope of having a few Exotic Encounters.
Video.title: "Exotic Encounters"

Video.summary: Willi and Tom Petry meet in Iowa with good friends, the Wrights to hunt turkeys on their farms. Willi and Tom drew the first season in Iowa and had the first kick at the cat for the spring. They had hunted deer with Cody and Cassie but neither had hunted turkeys in Iowa. They are hoping for a little luck with the gobblers, and they know they are going to have a good time trying.
Video.title: "Iowa Turkey, .410's and Reapers"

Video.summary: Josh and Alex explore the SS United States; the world‘s largest artificial reef.
Video.title: "Wrecked Em'"

Video.summary: Join Mark Peterson as he ventures into Croatia’s wild heart, hot on the trail of a legendary stag. In the midst of the rutting season’s deafening roars, tensions escalate as Mark closes the distance. - Equipped with his Gunwerks 7LRM, things get dicey as Mark is face to face with a roaring stag at 12 yards!
Video.title: "Roaring Stag at 12 Yards"

Video.summary: Capt. Jonathan Moss is heading offshore into the Gulf of America, eager to feed his offshore fishing obsession.
Video.title: "Offshore Obsession"

Video.summary: The bite’s dead during a neap tide, but Josh and Isaiah don’t back down. Watch them adapt, pivot, and turn a tough day into a hard-earned win. It’s not luck—it’s hustle.
Video.title: "Neap tide Hustle"

Video.summary: Welcome to Season 2 of Turkey Ninja presented by WoodHaven! It's opening day of youth season in Alabama and Mike and Jacob are ready to work on some longbeards! Right off the roost turkeys are in sight and put on quite a show for Jacob who is trying to punch a tag on his 39th "Shrutter"!
Video.title: "OPENING DAY 'SHRUTTER"

Video.summary: On this adventure, we tag along with the Disabled American Veterans of Minnesota "Outdoor Programs" as they travel to Texas for a Hog hunt. We also honor our next Wichita Kansas Scheels Hometown Hero, Will Rivera!
Video.title: "Dav of MN Texas Hog Hunt"

Video.summary: On this adventure, we travel to Sioux Narrows Ontario Canada for a week long trip with Time on the Water to Lake of the Woods. In this episode we share the stories of Vietnam Veteran Leon Merhkens, fire fighters Jeff Benson/Seth Beyer and founder/CEO of Time on the Water, Chris Hansen. In addition, the fishing is pretty good too! We also highlight Iowa City Scheels Hometown hero Nic Pruter.
Video.title: "Time on the Water"

Video.summary: Vietnam war veteran Don Weber recounts his amazing journey through survivors guilt, PTSD and starting over.
Video.title: "Don Weber"

Video.summary: U.S. Army veteran Carlos Powell tells of family sacrifice and fighting his post service “demons.”
Video.title: "Carlos Powell"

Video.summary: WoodHaven's Phillip Vanderpool pulls off one of the most incredible hunts of his career — taking down “Cyclops,” a 187” Kansas typical buck! This self-filmed hunt has it all: calling, decoying, and a shot from the ground. Watch as Phillip puts all his skills to the test and brings down one of his biggest bucks ever, completely captured on camera.
Video.title: "Self-Filmed Monster"

Video.summary: The WoodHaven crew travels south to hunt with Jay Scott Outdoors, bringing along Tyler Jordan for his first-ever Gould’s turkey hunt. What starts as a goal to tag one bird quickly turns into an unforgettable hunt in some of the best Gould’s turkey country in Mexico.
Video.title: "Gould's Turkey Part 1"

Video.summary: The Alabama turkey opener is a tradition we look forward to every single year. This season, Aaron and Nick from The Hunting Public joined us in camp to kick things off and experience the opener with us. Good friends, strong tradition, and some Alabama gobblers made for an unforgettable start to the season.
Video.title: "THP Comes To Town"

Video.summary: Mike Pentecost sets up a new stand on a creek crossing in Illinois that had been scouted for the past two years. It always heated up as the season progressed, and on his very first morning sit, the action unfolded fast. A good buck came cruising from the South in search of a doe. At 60 yards he started to turn away, but the Intimidator grunt call changed everything. With a few calls from the Intimidator, he spun around from 150 yards, worked a scrape at 40 yards, and walked right into range!
Video.title: "Right Spot, Right Time"

Video.summary: Mark and Lisa have a big flock of Rio turkeys right off the roost but have to make a bold move in the Oklahoma winds to get in on this big gobbler.
Video.title: "BIG FLOCK RIO's"

Video.summary: Capt. Mike heads to Venice, LA, to explore a unique bayou technique —beach fishing for redfish and black drum in the heart of the Gulf.
Video.title: "Redfish Lodge"

Video.summary: Capt. Mike and Dallas King take advantage of a powerful summer tide, hooking into an all-out redfish frenzy where the action never slows down.
Video.title: "King Tide"

Video.summary: Capt Josh and Johnny from Johnny Jigs head out with gorgeous weather on their side to hit some reefs and put the jigs to work!
Video.title: "Johnny Jigs"

Video.summary: Captain Josh and the fam head out to do some surf fishing to see what’s biting. Then ended the day building professional sand castles and playing goofy golf.
Video.title: "Family Fun"

Video.summary: Rock bottom and spoons? Sounds like a trip up to Steinhatchee to meet up with Captain Danny Allen!
Video.title: "Golden Boy"

Video.summary: Captain CA embarks on a expedition to the renowned Florida Sports Coast, accompanied by esteemed Bait Designer Jose Chavez, to conduct rigorous field tests on cutting-edge Z-MAN Products. This collaborative endeavor seeks to explore the efficacy and performance of the innovative fishing lures in real-world conditions, thereby advancing the understanding of optimal bait design.
Video.title: "Cantankerous Cobia"

Video.summary: Get ready for an extraordinary fishing adventure like no other as Captain C.A. Richardson sets sail to Jacksonville, Florida, to join forces with former NFL All Pro Lineman and avid outdoor enthusiast, Brandon Linder, on an unforgettable fishing expedition.
Video.title: "Tides and Gridiron"

Video.summary: Get ready for an adrenaline-pumping episode of Flats Class TV as we dive into the heart of East Coast Florida's angling paradise in "Project Mayhem." Join your host, Captain C.A. Richardson, on an unforgettable fishing expedition that promises not only exceptional angling action but also a dash of excitement that only the unpredictable waters of the East Coast can provide.
Video.title: "Project Mayhem"

Video.summary: Despite adverse conditions, Capt. Jonathan Moss and long time friend, Capt. Adam Whitson, are heading out on the water in search of the Crystal River Inshore Slam.
Video.title: "Weather or Not"

Video.summary: Central Florida’s St. John’s River is home to countless species of wildlife, making this sweet water estuary the perfect playground for a pair of river rats, Captains Jonathan Moss and Nick Clark.
Video.title: "River Rats"

Video.summary: With Montana in the rearview mirror the Flylords team crosses over into Idaho. In this leg of the trip they hit Blue-Ribbon trout waters with the folks from Henrys Fork Anglers and Silver Creek Outfitters. They also have a chance to sit down with the owners of these operations and hear what these shops and fly fishing mean to them. Before they hit the road to Utah the crew joins in on another conservation event at Payette Brewing Company.
Video.title: "SIGHT Casting RISING Trout in IDAHO"

Video.summary: It’s the final leg of the Fly Shop Tour and what better way to end it than with a trip down to see the crew at Fly Fish Food. We learn the unique story of how owners Clark “Cheech” Pierce & Curtis Fry started their shop and spend some time chasing hungry Cutthroat. Wrapping it all up the team puts on the final conservation event of the season with Fly Fish Food and Fish For Garbage, as well as spending some time behind the oars on the Green River with crew at Western Rivers Fly Shop.
Video.title: "The END of our 2500 Mile Road Trip"

Image.alt: "The END of our 2500 Mile Road Trip"
"The END of our 2500 Mile Road Trip"
"The END of our 2500 Mile Road Trip"

Video.summary: Travelling to Vancouver Island, Dan Cabela meets with tribal leader Chief Cootes to learn more about how the careful management of black bears offers economic benefits while maintaining cultural importance to his community.
Video.title: "Coastal Black Bear"

Video.summary: Turkey hunting in Texas hits differently — especially when it’s your first time behind the gun. In this week’s HuntStand TURKEY POSSE episode, Will Cooper introduces longtime best friends Aubry Gold and Chance Eckert to the world of spring turkey hunting in the Lone Star State. What starts as a casual hunt quickly turns into full-blown addiction as Rio Grande gobblers fire up, decoys get worked, and the Texas cedars come alive.
Video.title: "Texas Turkey: First Toms and a New Addiction"

Image.alt: "Texas Turkey: First Toms and a New Addiction"
"Texas Turkey: First Toms and a New Addiction"
"Texas Turkey: First Toms and a New Addiction"

Video.summary: Willi and friend, Rob Shaw hunt whitetails with their bows with Phire Creek Outdoors. They both hope Phire Creek and Kansas live up to their reputations of big whitetail bucks.
Video.title: "Kansas Giant Whitetails, Hits And Misses"

Video.summary: Join Austin and his friend DJ as they head afield in an attempt to double up on spring gobblers.
Video.title: The - "TWO Birds Before 10AM | Pennsylvania Turkey Hunting"

Image.alt: The - "TWO Birds Before 10AM | Pennsylvania Turkey Hunting"
The - "TWO Birds Before 10AM | Pennsylvania Turkey Hunting"
The - "TWO Birds Before 10AM | Pennsylvania Turkey Hunting"

Video.summary: About 2-3 months ago Mark purchased a 300+ acre farm in Kentucky. After running trail cameras and putting food plots on the farm, Mark now has the chance to get in the stand on opening day of Kentucky's bow season. There have been plenty of shooter bucks on the farm but only a few still are holding their velvet. Mark has his sights set on a Giant Kentucky Velvet Whitetail. The day before opening day Salt River puts on a celebration that features the @theswonbrothers.
Video.title: "Giant Kentucky Velvet Whitetails"

Video.summary: It's 2016 and Mark heads down to Kansas for an early season Muzzleloader Whitetail hunt with Salt River Outfitters. It was hot in the stand with little deer movement until the moon phase switched and - one of the bucks Mark had been looking for stepped out. Kansas is one of those states that has an early Muzzleloader season that opens in September, and if you can get the weather and the moon to align you can catch a big deer on his feet in daylight.
Video.title: "Giant 15 Point Kansas Whitetail, Early Season Hunt"

Image.alt: "Giant 15 Point Kansas Whitetail, Early Season Hunt"
"Giant 15 Point Kansas Whitetail, Early Season Hunt"
"Giant 15 Point Kansas Whitetail, Early Season Hunt"

Video.summary: Join Mark Peterson on an epic hunting adventure in the stunning landscapes of New Zealand as he goes after Whitetail deer. On this hunt, Mark was unable to get his firearms import permit in time to take his Gunwerks rifle, so he'd have to borrow a trusty rifle from his guide, Shawn. Get ready for heart-pounding action, breathtaking scenery, and valuable tips and tricks from a seasoned hunter.
Video.title: "Hunting Whitetail In New Zealand"

Video.summary: Mark continues his quest in New Zealand, but this time, after a Wapiti. On this hunt, Mark was unable to get his firearms import permit in time to take his Gunwerks rifle, so he'd again have to borrow from his guide, Shawn. Elk were first introduced to New Zealand in 1909 when President Roosevelt gifted a herd of 20 animals that were released on the South Island. Get ready to immerse yourself in the beauty of nature that New Zealand has to offer.
Video.title: "Hunting Wapiti in New Zealand"

Video.summary: Kyle takes to the water in a way few anglers dare, fishing from a Sea-Doo in search of the ancient lake sturgeon, a species that’s been around since the age of the dinosaurs and can live over 100 years. Hooking into one of North America’s largest freshwater fish from a personal watercraft proves to be a challenge unlike any other.
Video.title: "Extreme Lake Sturgeon"

Video.summary: Kyle and Jeff dive into the wild waters off Hawaii’s coast, coming face-to-face with sharks and graceful manta rays. The adrenaline peaks when Kyle takes a heart-pounding leap off a towering 40-foot cliff, making this adventure as daring as it is unforgettable.
Video.title: "Fishing Hawaii"

Video.summary: Kyle and Jeff explore the breathtaking Waipio Valley, the Hawaiian "Valley of the Kings," in search of prawns and bullfrogs. Their adventure continues up a massive volcano, hunting invasive rams, before ending with a feast of native fish paired with island fruits and spices.
Video.title: "Hunting Hawaii"

Video.summary: Kyle and Jeff hear about the dangers of fishing for Bluefin Tuna in the Atlantic Ocean from locals in Gloucester, Massachusetts. Then they head 30 miles out onto the open ocean with Darrin of Reel Respect Charters in the hopes of landing their own giant.
Video.title: "Gloucester Giant Bluefin"

Video.summary: Kyle and Jeff team up with their friends from Boss Shotshells in South Dakota for a high-energy upland hunt, pursuing wild pheasants with the help of skilled hunting dogs. The adventure takes a quirky turn with a visit to the iconic Corn Palace.
Video.title: "South Dakota Pheasant"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor run from Hawks Cay Resort up to Islamorada on a windy day to fish under the mangroves for Goliath grouper.
Video.title: "Goliaths Under the Bushes"

Video.summary: During this episode of Ship Shape TV Essentials the SSTV team is taking - the Marine Surplus Console for the Aqua sport project and giving it a much needed face lift. They meet the team at Boat Outfitters to find an acrylic covering board to fit the console and cover the pre existing holes. They then head over to meet with crew at AwlGrip to get some ideas on color matching the console with the Aqua sport.
Video.title: Essentials - "Refit on a Surplus Console"

Video.summary: Michael and Larken travel to South Carolina's Lowcountry for a massive barbecue charity event supporting children battling serious illness. After serving up smoke for a great cause, they spend an extra day in the marsh chasing redfish and speckled trout.
Video.title: "Holy Smokes"

Video.summary: Michael and Larken Letchworth set out on a new adventure, exploring food fishing and the great outdoors. This father son duo are making memories that will last a lifetime and will forever be etched in the digital world.
Video.title: "The Beginning"

Video.summary: In this episode we will dive the beautiful reefs and wrecks of Key Largo. We will showcase research that is being done in Key Largo to protect reefs worldwide.
Video.title: "Key Largo"

Video.summary: In Episode 10 Boat Trader heads to central Texas to explore boating culture in the lone star state. Second only to Alaska in size, Texas is known as the place where everything is bigger. From big college football games to endless oil fields and vast wind farms, Texas is home to more than 30 million Americans. But the state is about more than just big oil, college football and cattle farms - it's also home to some of the best boating in the country. Boasting a myriad of waterways, boaters here enjoy hundreds of lakes, rivers and streams that provide recreational opportunities, abundant wildlife and plenty of fishing. From Austin Texas to San Antonio host Ryan McVinney and the Boat Trader crew explore the laid-back Texas lake life including boat up restaurants along the Colorado River, watersports in Austin and the history of the waterways throughout the region. Boat manufacturers highlighted in this episode are Tige Boats and ATX Surf Boats, two premium Texas-based wake boat brands known for their high-quality, cutting-edge vessels that come equipped with advanced wake shaping technologies and equipment ideal for water skiing, wake surfing and wakeboarding. There's even a wake surfing dog!
Video.title: "Lone Star Boating: Exploring Central Texas Lakes & Rivers"

Image.alt: "Lone Star Boating: Exploring Central Texas Lakes & Rivers"
"Lone Star Boating: Exploring Central Texas Lakes & Rivers"
"Lone Star Boating: Exploring Central Texas Lakes & Rivers"

Video.summary: The National Wild Turkey Federation stands for much more than the wild turkey – it’s also about forging lifelong relationships and enjoying the kind of fellowship you can only find in turkey camp. Josh Dahlke joins NWTF Regional Director Kevin Hurst and friends to celebrate Spring in pursuit of Missouri longbeards.
Video.title: "Missouri Turkey Camp with NWTF"

Video.summary: If you ask Mark Peterson what his all time most memorable hunt is, he would likely say that it's anything he's done with his kids. That answer would quickly be followed up with "Blue Sheep Hunting in Pakistan, hands down". Here, Mark takes you back to 2017 to the first (and still only) filmed hunt for Blue Sheep in Northern Pakistan. It's ironic that his most challenging and physically brutal hunt is also the one that holds the number one spot in Mark's hunting career. Enjoy this second of three episodes as he pays tribute to Sheep Week, 2022. Click the link below to join and learn more about The Wild Sheep Foundation.
Video.title: "FIRST EVER ON FILM- Pakistan Blue Sheep Hunt"

Image.alt: "FIRST EVER ON FILM- Pakistan Blue Sheep Hunt"
"FIRST EVER ON FILM- Pakistan Blue Sheep Hunt"
"FIRST EVER ON FILM- Pakistan Blue Sheep Hunt"

Video.summary: Mark Scroggins and his family are in Oklahoma and spot a big gobbler from the road. They are pretty sure they can get in position to call him in but they have to be quick without being seen. His step-daughter Destiny is behind the trigger and Mark just about puts this ol gobbler in her lap!
Video.title: "HEART ATTACK TURKEY HUNT"

Video.summary: For someone like Capt. Cory who's out on the water most days of the year, it's easy to notice the little changes that show the stress on an ecosystem. Whether that's subtle movements of fish from lingering red tide blooms or large-scale impacts from high-volume Lak Okeechobee discharges, he sees it all, and he's ready for things to change. "I wish we would get to a point where we really put our foot down and do something about it before it's too late." - Watch this episode to learn more about what's going on and to see why Capt. Cory is so passionate about fighting to protect the places he loves. Visit https://captainsforcleanwater.org to join the fight.
Video.title: "" With Captain Cory McGuire
